title: amaryllis × pallescens
intro: Amaryllis × pallescens is a hybrid plant within the Amaryllidaceae family, derived from the combination of Amaryllis belladonna and Amaryllis paradisicola. This plant contains alkaloids, specifically lycorine, which have been found to possess medicinal properties. The health benefits of Amaryllis × pallescens include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antitumor activities. Traditionally, the plant's bulb is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and extracts to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is necessary to fully understand its therapeutic applications.
